raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which review focus should I prioritize: A) technical claims/logic and evidence alignment, B) language and clarity editing, or C) conceptual framing and section structure?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Assess the internal coherence of guarantees and assumptions (e.g., partitions vs eventual consistency) without judging experiment design.",
    "R1.3": "Check whether stated claims (latency, availability, bounded staleness) are supported by the reported measurements and bounds as written.",
    "R1.6": "Evaluate sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision in the presented text.",
    "RX.1": "Flag where assumptions or uncertainties (e.g., failure models, testbed scope) should be explicitly disclosed.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against overbroad claims by aligning strength of language (e.g., 'guarantees') with the evidence provided in this section.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the review limited to evaluating this section rather than redesigning the system or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid feedback that could encourage misrepresenting resilience or consistency guarantees in ways that could cause downstream harm.",
    "RX.5": "Base all review points on claims traceable to the provided text and its referenced sections."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which review focus should I prioritize: A) technical claims/logic and evidence alignment, B) language and clarity editing, or C) conceptual framing and section structure?

rationale.R1.2: Assess the internal coherence of guarantees and assumptions (e.g., partitions vs eventual consistency) without judging experiment design.
rationale.R1.3: Check whether stated claims (latency, availability, bounded staleness) are supported by the reported measurements and bounds as written.
rationale.R1.6: Evaluate sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision in the presented text.
rationale.RX.1: Flag where assumptions or uncertainties (e.g., failure models, testbed scope) should be explicitly disclosed.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against overbroad claims by aligning strength of language (e.g., 'guarantees') with the evidence provided in this section.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the review limited to evaluating this section rather than redesigning the system or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid feedback that could encourage misrepresenting resilience or consistency guarantees in ways that could cause downstream harm.
rationale.RX.5: Base all review points on claims traceable to the provided text and its referenced sections.
